name: Neapolitan Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Thaw and drain strawberries thoroughly.
2. Soften cream cheese.
3. Prepare Chocolate crumb crust: Heat oven to 350°F. In a small bowl, stir together 1 1/2 cups vanilla wafer crumbs (about 45 wafers), 1/2 cup powdered sugar, 1/4 cup cocoa, and 1/3 cup melted butter or margarine. Press mixture into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan. Bake for 8 minutes.
4. In a blender or food processor, puree strawberries to yield 1/2 cup; set aside.
5. Microwave chocolate chips at High for 1 1/2 minutes; stir. If necessary, microwave at High an additional 15 seconds at a time, stirring after each heating, just until chips are melted when stirred; set aside.
6. In a large mixing bowl, beat cream cheese with sugar until smooth. Blend in eggs, sour cream, flour, vanilla, salt, and red food color (if desired); beat until smooth.
7. In a separate bowl, stir together 1 1/3 cups batter and remaining 1 teaspoon flour with strawberries; add if desired.
8. Pour strawberry batter into the crust.
9. Carefully spoon 2 cups vanilla batter over the strawberry batter; smooth the surface.
10. Stir melted chocolate into the remaining batter; carefully smooth over the vanilla batter, smoothing the surface.
11. Bake at 350°F for 55 to 60 minutes or until the center is almost set. Remove from oven and let cool on a cooling rack for 30 minutes.
12. Cool completely to room temperature. Refrigerate several hours or overnight. Loosen cake from the rim of the pan, then refrigerate leftovers.
